annotations.label: Area

anchor.snippet: Area is the measure of a region

annotations.note: Area as the "measure of a region" is captured by the general measure-theoretic framework (e.g. 2D Lebesgue volume), but there is no dedicated "area" definition.

annotations.label: Surface area

anchor.snippet: surface area refers to the area of an open surface

annotations.note: 2D Hausdorff measure generalizes the notion of surface area, but surface area of embedded surfaces is not defined as such.

annotations.label: Unit square has area one

anchor.snippet: the unit square is defined to have area one

annotations.note: Lebesgue volume is normalized so that the unit box [0,1]^n has measure 1, a special case of the box-volume formula.

annotations.label: Axiomatic area function

anchor.snippet: can be defined as a function from a collection

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Measure` is the general axiomatic (countably-additive, nonneg) set function; the specific Jordan-style area axioms are not separately bundled.

annotations.label: Non-negativity of area

anchor.snippet: For all S in M

annotations.note: Measures are ℝ≥0∞-valued, and `measureReal_nonneg` gives 0 ≤ μ.real s exactly.

annotations.label: Inclusion-exclusion additivity

anchor.snippet: If S and T are in M then so are

annotations.note: `measure_union_add_inter` states μ(S∪T)+μ(S∩T)=μ S+μ T, equivalent to two-set inclusion-exclusion.

annotations.label: Area of set difference

anchor.snippet: with S ⊆ T then

annotations.note: `measure_diff` gives μ(S₁\S₂)=μ S₁−μ S₂ for measurable S₂⊆S₁ of finite measure.

annotations.label: Congruence invariance of area

anchor.snippet: S is congruent to T then

annotations.note: Volume is preserved by linear isometries (rotations/reflections) and by translations (Haar invariance), but full affine-congruence invariance is not a single declaration.

annotations.label: Area of a rectangle axiom

anchor.snippet: Every rectangle R is in M

annotations.note: The box-volume lemma gives volume of a product of intervals as the product of side lengths, i.e. the rectangle-area axiom in 2D.

annotations.label: Step region squeeze

anchor.snippet: A step region is formed from a finite union of adjacent rectangles

annotations.note: No formalization of step-region (finite rectangle union) inner/outer squeezing of area exists, though box-integration infrastructure is present.

annotations.label: Existence of area function

anchor.snippet: It can be proved that such an area function actually exists

annotations.note: Lebesgue/Haar measure is constructed (so an area function exists), but existence/uniqueness specifically under the Jordan-area axioms is not proved.

annotations.label: Unit of area from unit of length

anchor.snippet: Every unit of length has a corresponding unit of area

annotations.note: Physical units of measurement are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area of a 1-metre square

anchor.snippet: Calculation of the area of a square whose length and width are 1 metre

annotations.note: Unit-based area conversions are not formalized (the bare 1×1=1 is a trivial instance of the box-volume formula).

annotations.label: Disk area proportional to diameter squared

anchor.snippet: the area of a disk (the region enclosed by a circle) is proportional to the square of its diameter

annotations.note: Volume of a 2D ball equals π·r², hence is proportional to the square of the diameter.

annotations.label: Archimedes' circle-triangle area

anchor.snippet: the area inside a circle is equal to that of a right triangle whose base has the length of the circle's circumference

annotations.note: The identity area = ½·circumference·radius is not formalized (circle circumference/perimeter is not available as a length).

annotations.label: Triangle is half a parallelogram (Euclid)

anchor.snippet: the area of a triangle is half that of a parallelogram with the same base and height

annotations.note: No triangle-vs-parallelogram area relation is formalized.

annotations.label: Heron's formula

anchor.snippet: Heron's formula for the area of a triangle in terms of its sides

annotations.note: Heron's formula is not in Mathlib (Geometry.Euclidean.Triangle has only the laws of cosines/sines).

annotations.label: Triangle area one-half base times height

anchor.snippet: the area of a triangle as one-half the base times the height

annotations.note: The ½·base·height triangle-area formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Brahmagupta's formula

anchor.snippet: for the area of a cyclic quadrilateral

annotations.note: Only the algebraic Brahmagupta(-Fibonacci) identity exists (Algebra.Ring.Identities); the cyclic-quadrilateral area formula is absent.

annotations.label: Bretschneider's formula

anchor.snippet: Bretschneider's formula , for the area of any quadrilateral

annotations.note: Bretschneider's quadrilateral area formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Surveyor's formula (general polygon)

anchor.snippet: the surveyor's formula for the area of any polygon with known vertex locations

annotations.note: The surveyor's/shoelace polygon-area formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Surveyor's formula for a simple polygon

anchor.snippet: the area is given by the surveyor's formula

annotations.note: No shoelace/surveyor formula for simple polygons exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area of a rectangle

anchor.snippet: Given a rectangle with length l and width w , the formula for the area is

annotations.note: Box volume = product of side lengths gives A = l·w for a 2D rectangle.

annotations.label: Area of a square

anchor.snippet: the area of a square with side length s is given by the formula

annotations.note: A = s² is the equal-sides special case of the box-volume formula.

annotations.label: Area of a parallelogram

anchor.snippet: the area of the parallelogram is the same as the area of the rectangle

annotations.note: `addHaar_parallelepiped` gives the measure of a parallelepiped (incl. 2D parallelogram) as |det| of the spanning vectors; the base×height form is not separately stated.

annotations.label: Triangle is half its parallelogram

anchor.snippet: the area of each triangle is half the area of the parallelogram

annotations.note: The triangle-is-half-its-parallelogram dissection result is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: the area of each triangle is half the area of the parallelogram

annotations.label: Area of a circle

anchor.snippet: the total area of the circle is

annotations.note: Volume of a 2D ball of radius r is exactly π·r² (also `Complex.volume_ball`).

annotations.label: Area of an ellipse

anchor.snippet: for an ellipse with semi-major and semi-minor axes x and y the formula is

annotations.note: The ellipse area formula π·a·b is not formalized (no ellipse region defined).

anchors.snippet: for an ellipse with semi-major and semi-minor axes x and y the formula is

annotations.label: Surface area of a sphere

anchor.snippet: The formula for the surface area of a sphere was first obtained by Archimedes

annotations.note: The 4π·r² sphere surface area is not formalized (only the volume of the 3-ball via `volume_ball_fin_three`).

annotations.label: Triangle area from base and height

anchor.snippet: where B is any side, and h is the distance from the line on which B lies

annotations.note: The base-times-height triangle-area formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Heron's formula

anchor.snippet: If the lengths of the three sides are known then Heron's formula can be used

annotations.note: Heron's formula is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Triangle area from two sides and angle

anchor.snippet: If an angle and its two included sides are given, the area is

annotations.note: The ½·a·b·sin C triangle-area formula is not formalized (areaForm gives the signed parallelogram area of two vectors but no triangle-area statement).

annotations.label: Shoelace formula

anchor.snippet: This formula is also known as the shoelace formula

annotations.note: The shoelace formula is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pick's theorem

anchor.snippet: This result is known as Pick's theorem

annotations.note: Pick's theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area under a curve

anchor.snippet: The area between a positive-valued curve and the horizontal axis

annotations.note: Area between a positive curve and the axis is the f=0 case of the region-between-graphs integral.

anchors.snippet: The area between a positive-valued curve and the horizontal axis

annotations.label: Area between two graphs

anchor.snippet: The area between the graphs of two functions is equal to the integral of one function

annotations.note: `volume_regionBetween_eq_integral` gives the measure of the region between f and g as ∫(g−f).

anchors.snippet: The area between the graphs of two functions is equal to the integral of one function

annotations.label: Area in polar coordinates

anchor.snippet: expressed in polar coordinates is

annotations.note: The polar-area formula ½∫r²dθ is not formalized (`polarCoord` provides only a change-of-variables Jacobian).

annotations.label: Area enclosed by a parametric curve

anchor.snippet: The area enclosed by a parametric curve

annotations.note: The Green's-theorem area formula for a parametric curve is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: The area enclosed by a parametric curve

annotations.label: Bounded area between two quadratics

anchor.section: Bounded area between two quadratic functions
anchor.snippet: To find the bounded area between two quadratic functions

annotations.note: The specific closed-form area between two quadratics is not formalized (general region-between integral exists).

anchors.section: Bounded area between two quadratic functions
anchors.snippet: To find the bounded area between two quadratic functions

annotations.label: Surface area of a cone

anchor.snippet: where r is the radius of the circular base, and h is the height

annotations.note: The cone surface-area formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Surface area of a cube

anchor.snippet: where s is the length of an edge

annotations.note: The cube surface-area formula 6s² is not formalized.

annotations.label: Surface area of a cylinder

anchor.snippet: where r is the radius of a base and h is the height

annotations.note: The cylinder surface-area formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Surface area of a prism

anchor.snippet: where B is the area of a base, P is the perimeter of a base, and h is the height of the prism

annotations.note: The prism surface-area formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Surface area of a pyramid

anchor.snippet: where B is the area of the base, P is the perimeter of the base, and L is the length of the slant

annotations.note: The pyramid surface-area formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Surface area of a rectangular prism

anchor.snippet: is the length, w is the width, and h is the height

annotations.note: The rectangular-prism surface-area formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Surface area of a graph

anchor.snippet: The general formula for the surface area of the graph of a continuously differentiable function

annotations.note: The ∫√(1+|∇f|²) graph surface-area formula is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: The general formula for the surface area of the graph of a continuously differentiable function

annotations.label: Area of a parametric surface

anchor.snippet: An even more general formula for the area of the graph of a parametric surface

annotations.note: The parametric-surface area formula is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: An even more general formula for the area of the graph of a parametric surface

annotations.label: Isoperimetric inequality

anchor.snippet: The isoperimetric inequality states that, for a closed curve of length L

annotations.note: The isoperimetric inequality is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Circle area-to-circumference ratio

anchor.snippet: the ratio of the area to the circumference

annotations.note: The area/circumference = r/2 ratio is not formalized (no circle circumference).

annotations.label: Area of a regular polygon

anchor.snippet: The area of a regular polygon is half its perimeter times the apothem

annotations.note: The regular-polygon area formula (½·perimeter·apothem) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fractal dimension scaling

anchor.snippet: Doubling the edge lengths of a polygon multiplies its area by four

annotations.note: `addHaar_smul` gives μ(r•s)=|r|^dim·μ s, so in 2D doubling lengths scales area by 4; the fractal (non-integer) dimension aspect is not formalized.

annotations.label: Infinitude of triangle area bisectors

anchor.snippet: There are an infinitude of lines that bisect the area of a triangle

annotations.note: Results on area-bisecting lines of a triangle are not formalized.

annotations.label: Area-and-perimeter bisector through incenter

anchor.snippet: splits both the triangle's area and its perimeter in half goes through the triangle's incenter

annotations.note: This area-and-perimeter bisector/incenter result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Parallelogram midpoint bisector

anchor.snippet: Any line through the midpoint of a parallelogram bisects the area

annotations.note: The parallelogram-midpoint area-bisector result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Ellipse area bisectors through center

anchor.snippet: All area bisectors of a circle or other ellipse go through the center

annotations.note: The circle/ellipse area-bisector-through-center result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Minimal surface

anchor.snippet: the surface of least area spanning

annotations.note: Minimal surfaces / the Plateau problem are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Circle maximizes area for given perimeter

anchor.snippet: The circle has the largest area of any two-dimensional object having the same perimeter

annotations.note: This isoperimetric optimality of the circle is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cyclic polygon maximizes area

anchor.snippet: A cyclic polygon (one inscribed in a circle) has the largest area of any polygon

annotations.note: The cyclic-polygon area-maximization result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Isoperimetric inequality for triangles

anchor.snippet: the triangle of greatest area among all those with a given perimeter is equilateral

annotations.note: The equilateral-maximizes-area-for-given-perimeter result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Equilateral triangle extremal in a circle

anchor.snippet: The triangle of largest area of all those inscribed in a given circle is equilateral

annotations.note: The inscribed-triangle area-maximization result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Incircle-to-triangle area ratio maximized

anchor.snippet: The ratio of the area of the incircle to the area of an equilateral triangle

annotations.note: The incircle-to-triangle area-ratio extremality is not formalized.

annotations.label: Area-to-perimeter-squared ratio maximized

anchor.snippet: The ratio of the area to the square of the perimeter of an equilateral triangle

annotations.note: The area-to-perimeter² extremality among triangles is not formalized.
